text.title: ELK Stack Architecture and Deployment Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Single-node cluster runs all roles; cluster red at first burst; master OOMs from data-side pressure.
antipattern.root-cause: Author scaled the dev single-node config to prod.
antipattern.fix: Split into master / data / coordinating role nodes per Elastic best-practice.

antipattern.symptom: 2 master nodes; network partition → both think they're elected → divergent state when partition heals.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought 2 masters = HA; missed quorum requirement.
antipattern.fix: Add a 3rd dedicated master node. Set discovery.zen.minimum_master_nodes = 2 (legacy) or use the cluster-coordination API.

antipattern.symptom: All data on SSD; storage cost 5x what it should be; team plans deletion of old data instead of moving to warm.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped tier setup because 'we'll add later'; ILM never came.
antipattern.fix: Add data-warm + data-cold nodes; create ILM policy that rolls data hot→warm→cold→delete based on age.

antipattern.symptom: Heap set to 64 GB; node crashes on first heavy aggregation; lose compressed oops benefit.
antipattern.fix: Set heap to min(50% of RAM, 31 GB). Use the remaining RAM for OS file cache (Lucene loves it).
